one_time_description: Equity income from Simpcw Resources LLP (government business enterprise) of $72,153,567 representing logging, gravel and environmental contract operations - significantly higher than prior year $27,822,340
economic_notes: Simpcw Resources LLP engaged in logging, gravel operations and environmental contract work with revenues of $274,120,033 and net income of $72,160,783. Simpcw First Nation holds 99.99% limited partner interest. Multi-purpose facility under construction with budget of $27,636,980, with $1,676,753 incurred to date and $13,744,000 in secured grants. Various Impact Benefit Agreements and Mutual Benefits Agreements with Kinder Morgan and Trans Mountain Pipeline.
notes: Fiscal year end March 31, 2022. Massive surplus driven almost entirely by equity income from Simpcw Resources LLP ($72.15M), a GBE engaged in logging and gravel operations - this is the modified equity method GBE. Consolidated entity includes Simpcw Holdings Limited (subsidiary) and four internal funds. Two GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: Simpcw Resources LLP and Simpcw Resources (2020) LLP. Net financial assets of $139.9M reflects large GBE investment on balance sheet. Wages and benefits from segmented statement: $4,585,899 (2022). Annual debt service reflects mortgage principal repayments only ($51,813 mortgages). No remuneration schedule included in extracted text. OSR calculation includes GBE income and gaming revenue sharing as own-source. Total government transfers: ISC $4,319,456 + FNHA $1,638,396 + CMHC $118,204 + Province BC $3,737,050 + DFO $159,197 = $9,972,303.
